body:
Hey Yves, checking in on where things stand with the Ambervale group arriving Friday. Last I heard we were waiting on the final rooming list and headcount from their coordinator — has that come through yet?

Let me know so I can lock the welcome amenities and brief the front desk before Thursday's ops huddle.

Jess
snippet: Hey Yves, checking in on where things stand with the Ambervale group arriving...
subject: Ambervale group arrival

body:
Hey Jess — yes, the rooming list landed this morning, finally. Twenty-two rooms, four suites, arrivals staggered Friday afternoon. I've got the welcome baskets sorted and a small catered reception going in the Cedar Room at 6.

One snag: suite 1412 has a late checkout ahead of them so housekeeping's tight. I'll flag it to the front desk and update the board now. We're in good shape for Thursday.

Yves
snippet: Hey Jess — yes, the rooming list landed this morning, finally. Twenty-two roo...
subject: Re: Ambervale group arrival

body:
Yves, drafting the agenda for Thursday's ops huddle. So far I have: guest feedback review, holiday booking outlook, and a quick front-desk staffing update. Anything you want me to add from your side?

Also, are you handling the run-of-show or should I? Happy to take it if you're slammed.

Jess
snippet: Yves, drafting the agenda for Thursday's ops huddle. So far I have: guest fee...
subject: Ops huddle Thursday

body:
That looks solid. I'd add a quick slot for the Ambervale group arrival — maybe five minutes — since they land Friday and the desk will want the rundown.

I can own the run-of-show. I'll build off last month's and send you the draft tomorrow so you can drop in your sections.

Yves
snippet: That looks solid. I'd add a quick slot for the Ambervale group arrival — mayb...
subject: Re: Ops huddle Thursday

body:
Perfect, I'll slot Ambervale right after guest feedback — that order makes sense. Send the run-of-show whenever it's ready, no rush before tomorrow afternoon.

One more thing: should we keep the 30-minute open questions buffer at the end or skip it? Last two huddles we wrapped early and people just filed out.

Jess
snippet: Perfect, I'll slot Ambervale right after guest feedback — that order makes se...
subject: Re: Ops huddle Thursday

body:
Hey Yves, ta mère mentioned you've got the Ambervale catering pitch coming up. Have you started thinking about how you want to walk in? These first meetings really set how a client values you for the whole contract.

Back when I ran the restaurant, having two or three clear asks ready made all the difference. Are you pushing for the standing weekly lunch account this round?

Papa
snippet: Hey Yves, ta mère mentioned you've got the Ambervale catering pitch coming up...
subject: Ambervale pitch prep

body:
Oui Papa — the pitch is next Wednesday. I've been pulling together a list of jobs we've nailed this season and I do plan to bring up the standing weekly account again. Andrea seemed more open to it last time we talked informally.

The thing I'm unsure about is whether to push on pricing in the same meeting or save it for a separate conversation. What do you think?

Yves
snippet: Oui Papa — the pitch is next Wednesday. I've been pulling together a list of ...
subject: Re: Ambervale pitch prep

body:
Honestly, I'd separate them. Pitch the menu and the service first, let her sit with it, and circle back to per-guest pricing in a follow-up. Doing both at once can feel purely transactional even when the timing is perfectly reasonable.

And come in with specifics — headcounts, turnaround times, the corporate lunches you've already pulled off without a hiccup. Andrea sounds like someone who responds to numbers over a sales pitch.

Call me after if you want to do a dry run. Proud of you for going after it.

Papa
snippet: Honestly, I'd separate them. Pitch the menu and the service first, let her si...
subject: Re: Ambervale pitch prep

body:
That's really helpful, merci. I'll keep pricing for the follow-up — you're right, it's cleaner that way. I've got a solid list, including the forty-cover Ambervale lunch we turned around in two days and the tourtière spread for their holiday open house.

Might take you up on that dry run Sunday evening if you're free. I'll text you beforehand.

Yves
snippet: That's really helpful, merci. I'll keep pricing for the follow-up — you're ri...
subject: Re: Ambervale pitch prep

body:
Yves, heads up — I got a notice that the food handler certification renewal deadline is coming up next month. Not sure if you're on the same cycle since Cuisine du Nord runs under the county, but worth checking.

Log into the health department portal and see if you have anything pending. It's usually about 90 minutes of modules total.

Papa
snippet: Yves, heads up — I got a notice that the food handler certification renewal d...
subject: Cert renewal reminder

body:
Good call, I completely forgot about that. Logging in now to check where I stand. Last year I did it at 11pm the night before the deadline, so I'm trying to do better this time.

Yves
snippet: Good call, I completely forgot about that. Logging in now to check where I st...
subject: Re: Cert renewal reminder

body:
Ha, sounds about right. The modules haven't changed much from last year so it should go quick. There's a new allergen-handling one though — it's about 25 minutes on its own.

Let me know if the portal login gives you trouble. I had to reset my password last week.

Papa
snippet: Ha, sounds about right. The modules haven't changed much from last year so it...
subject: Re: Cert renewal reminder

body:
Logged in — I've got three modules pending including the new allergen one. Going to knock out two tonight and finish the rest this weekend. Deadline's fine.

Portal login worked no problem, they must have sorted whatever was going on.

Yves
snippet: Logged in — I've got three modules pending including the new allergen one. Go...
subject: Re: Cert renewal reminder

body:
Good. Once you finish you'll get the certificate by email — save it with the business license folder so it's on file for the next event contract.

And let me know when you're done. Maybe we finally do that lunch we keep pushing. I'll take you for poutine at that place on Hawthorne.

Papa
snippet: Good. Once you finish you'll get the certificate by email — save it with the ...
subject: Re: Cert renewal reminder

body:
Hey babe, I know this week's slammed for both of us but I feel like we've barely crossed paths outside the apartment. Any chance you can break away for lunch one day? I'm flexible Wednesday or Friday between my shifts.

There's that new ramen spot on Division I've been dying to try.

Sienna
snippet: Hey babe, I know this week's slammed for both of us but I feel like we've bar...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Friday works perfectly — I've got a light afternoon, no events, so no rush to get back. Let's do the ramen spot, I've been curious about it too.

Meet there at 12:30? I'll text you the address in case you haven't saved it yet.

Yves
snippet: Friday works perfectly — I've got a light afternoon, no events, so no rush to...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey! I know we talk every day but I kind of miss having an actual dedicated catch-up with you. I've got a gap from 10:30 to noon on Tuesday — want to grab coffee at Coava or somewhere closer to the hotel?

Nothing agenda-y, just us.

Sienna
snippet: Hey! I know we talk every day but I kind of miss having an actual dedicated c...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Tuesday's perfect. 10:30 works and Coava is great — I can walk over from the Hawthorne Grand in ten minutes. I love that you formally scheduled a catch-up with your own boyfriend, very on-brand for you.

Are we doing pour-overs or going full pastry situation?

Yves
snippet: Tuesday's perfect. 10:30 works and Coava is great — I can walk over from the ...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Full pastry situation, obviously. I'm thinking we split the almond croissant and then argue about it like we always do.

Also I've got something I want to tell you in person — nothing bad, I promise! Just something I've been turning over. See you Tuesday.

Sienna
snippet: Full pastry situation, obviously. I'm thinking we split the almond croissant ...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Okay so Mara's birthday is the 12th and I really want to do something nice for her. I was thinking brunch at that place on Mississippi — the one with the bottomless mimosas and the shakshuka you love. We could do a Saturday, maybe the 11th so she's not wrecked after her actual birthday night out.

Are you free, and would you be down to help me plan it?

Sienna
snippet: Okay so Mara's birthday is the 12th and I really want to do something nice fo...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
The 11th works for me. That place is great and Mara would love it — bottomless mimosas plus shakshuka is genuinely a perfect birthday morning.

Do you want me to handle the reservation or are you on it? We should book soon if it's a weekend.

Yves
snippet: The 11th works for me. That place is great and Mara would love it — bottomles...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Can you make the reservation? There are about eight of us — me, you, Mara, Priya, Dev, Cam, and maybe two others TBD. Book for ten to be safe and we can adjust closer to the date.

I'll handle the cake. I found a bakery on Alberta that does custom tres leches and I know Mara will absolutely lose it.

Sienna
snippet: Can you make the reservation? There are about eight of us — me, you, Mara, Pr...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Done — reservation confirmed for ten at 11am on the 11th. They had a great slot, must be our lucky day.

The tres leches sounds incredible. Let me know what I owe for that and I'll Venmo you. This is going to be such a good birthday for her.

Yves
snippet: Done — reservation confirmed for ten at 11am on the 11th. They had a great sl...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Yves, the family phone plan is up for renewal next month and I've been comparing options. Our carrier is raising rates by about $20 per line which adds up fast across the family. I found two competitors that look decent — one's $15 cheaper per line but has weaker coverage out past the metro.

Since you're on the road more than the rest of us with deliveries, what does your coverage usually look like?

Papa
snippet: Yves, the family phone plan is up for renewal next month and I've been compar...
subject: Phone plan renewal

body:
Honestly coverage matters more to me than I'd like — I run deliveries out toward Hillsboro and the wineries for events, and there are stretches where signal just drops. That said, $15 a line is real money.

Which carriers are you looking at? Some of the smaller ones piggyback on the same towers as the majors now, so the gap isn't always as big as the maps suggest.

Yves
snippet: Honestly coverage matters more to me than I'd like — I run deliveries out tow...
subject: Re: Phone plan renewal

body:
I was looking at Visible and Mint mainly. Visible runs on the Verizon network so coverage should be close to what we have now. Mint's on T-Mobile, which has improved a lot but I've heard mixed things out in the sticks.

Visible might be the sweet spot — comparable coverage, real savings. I'll look at the international data too, since ta mère and I are hoping to get up to Montréal in the fall to see Nathalie.

Papa
snippet: I was looking at Visible and Mint mainly. Visible runs on the Verizon network...
subject: Re: Phone plan renewal

body:
Visible sounds good if it's truly the Verizon backbone. Check the international roaming closely though — some of these carriers charge a fortune for Canada or push you to a higher tier. Might change the math.

If you want, I can put together a comparison tonight and send it over. You've managed this for years, let me take something off your plate.

Yves
snippet: Visible sounds good if it's truly the Verizon backbone. Check the internation...
subject: Re: Phone plan renewal

body:
That'd be great, merci. Focus on the Canada add-on specifically — we'd be in Montréal about two weeks.

If the international piece makes Visible pricier than expected, also check whether our current carrier has a loyalty deal. They sometimes hand out retention offers when you call to cancel.

Papa
snippet: That'd be great, merci. Focus on the Canada add-on specifically — we'd be in ...
subject: Re: Phone plan renewal

body:
Yves, I got an invoice from Rose City Self Storage for $340 — that's for the unit you've been keeping the catering gear in under our account. I thought you cleared it out in the spring. Is this a billing lag or is there still something in there?

Let me know so I can either dispute it or just pass the charge to you.

Papa
snippet: Yves, I got an invoice from Rose City Self Storage for $340 — that's for the ...
subject: Storage invoice question

body:
Sorry about that — I did pull most of the gear in the spring but I left a couple of chafing racks behind meaning to grab them the next weekend and completely forgot. I'll go this Saturday and clear it out for good.

Go ahead and pass the $340 to me, that's entirely my fault. I'll Venmo you right now.

Yves
snippet: Sorry about that — I did pull most of the gear in the spring but I left a cou...
subject: Re: Storage invoice question

body:
Hey Yves, I know your lease is up in September. Have you heard from your landlord yet about renewal terms? The Beaverton rental market's been rough and I want to make sure you're not getting squeezed without time to look at alternatives.

If the increase is steep it might actually be worth looking at buying, depending on where rates land. Just something to chew on.

Papa
snippet: Hey Yves, I know your lease is up in September. Have you heard from your land...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
I got the renewal offer last week — they're asking for a 9% bump, which is steep but honestly not as bad as I feared given what's happening around here. I'm going to try to talk them down to around 6% and see what they say.

On the buying front, I've been thinking about it more seriously lately, especially as things get more serious with Sienna. Not quite there yet, but I'd love to walk through the numbers with you sometime soon.

Yves
snippet: I got the renewal offer last week — they're asking for a 9% bump, which is st...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
9% is annoying but negotiable — push back and mention you've been a reliable long-term tenant. Landlords hate a vacancy more than a small concession.

And yes, let's definitely walk through the buying math whenever you're ready. The down payment piece is manageable with some planning, and I have thoughts on that. No pressure on timing — just know the conversation's there.

Papa
snippet: 9% is annoying but negotiable — push back and mention you've been a reliable ...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Yves, forwarding a couple of things that caught my eye this week. First is an Eater piece on the boom in private-event and corporate catering — given what you're building with Cuisine du Nord, I thought it was relevant. Second is a Bon Appétit feature on Québécois cooking having a moment, tourtière and all.

Eater — The catering boom
Bon Appétit — Québec on the menu

Papa
snippet: Yves, forwarding a couple of things that caught my eye this week. First is an...
subject: Some reading for you this week

body:
The Eater piece is great — I sent it to Sienna and it sparked a whole conversation. We've been talking a lot about leaning harder into the corporate lunch side, so the timing was perfect.

Haven't gotten to the Bon Appétit one yet but I'm saving it for the weekend.

Yves
snippet: The Eater piece is great — I sent it to Sienna and it sparked a whole convers...
subject: Re: Some reading for you this week

body:
Glad it landed. I've been reading more food coverage lately, trying to keep up with what you're building. Ta mère thinks I'm turning into a critic, but I just want to hold an intelligent conversation with my son over dinner.

I'll keep forwarding when I spot something good. Tell me which topics are most useful and I'll filter better.

Papa
snippet: Glad it landed. I've been reading more food coverage lately, trying to keep u...
subject: Re: Some reading for you this week

body:
Ha, tell Maman the critic arc is complete — you asked me three genuinely sharp questions about commissary kitchens at Christmas.

Most useful for me right now: corporate catering trends, local sourcing, and anything on the holiday-season rush. Those are basically the three things keeping me busy this year.

Yves
snippet: Ha, tell Maman the critic arc is complete — you asked me three genuinely shar...
subject: Re: Some reading for you this week

body:
Yves, just confirming — am I still grabbing the supplier pickup Tuesday and running the Hawthorne Grand delivery Thursday this week? Ta mère has her thing Tuesday so she won't be around, but I can handle both days solo.

Also, what time does the Thursday event load-out usually wrap? Last week we ended up waiting almost twenty minutes past the posted time.

Papa
snippet: Yves, just confirming — am I still grabbing the supplier pickup Tuesday and r...
subject: Schedule check — deliveries

body:
Yes, Tuesday and Thursday still work great — merci, Papa. The load-out officially ends at 5:30, but the venue's been running 10 to 15 minutes over lately, so I'd plan for 5:45 to be safe.

If you've got time Thursday, you could also swing by the market stall and grab the empty chafers. No pressure though — I can get them over the weekend if it's too much.

Yves
snippet: Yes, Tuesday and Thursday still work great — merci, Papa. The load-out offici...
subject: Re: Schedule check — deliveries

body:
The stall is no problem at all, I'm happy to. I always end up chatting with the other vendors anyway — I learn more about this business from one Saturday at the market than from anything you try to explain to me.

I'll throw a cooler in the truck so the delivery trays stay cold on the drive. Last time the traffic on the 26 was brutal.

Papa
snippet: The stall is no problem at all, I'm happy to. I always end up chatting with t...
subject: Re: Schedule check — deliveries

body:
The cooler idea is smart — that drive in afternoon traffic is no joke. Trays need to stay under 40 the whole way or the client gets twitchy, and rightly so.

Merci again for doing this. It makes such a difference on a slammed week. You're basically my best driver and you work for poutine.

Yves
snippet: The cooler idea is smart — that drive in afternoon traffic is no joke. Trays ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — deliveries

body:
Poutine is a fair wage, I'll take it. You're building something good here, Yves — lending a hand is never a chore.

If you want, I can also cover next week while Sienna's pulling doubles at Copperstone. Just send me the schedule and I'll make myself available. It's what papas are for.

Papa
snippet: Poutine is a fair wage, I'll take it. You're building something good here, Yv...
subject: Re: Schedule check — deliveries

body:
Yves, ta mère and I have been talking about a long weekend at the coast before the fall rush really hits. We were thinking the Oregon coast — maybe four or five days somewhere around Cannon Beach. Nothing complicated, just a rental house, the beach, and good food.

Are you and Sienna in? I need to know before I start looking at houses so I get the right size.

Papa
snippet: Yves, ta mère and I have been talking about a long weekend at the coast befor...
subject: Coast getaway planning

body:
Yes, count us in — Cannon Beach sounds perfect, and honestly Sienna and I could both use a few days where nobody's catering anything. I'll double-check her shift schedule but I'm 99% sure she can swing it.

Go ahead and plan for the four of us. Send me what you find on rentals and I want to split the cost with you.

Yves
snippet: Yes, count us in — Cannon Beach sounds perfect, and honestly Sienna and I cou...
subject: Re: Coast getaway planning

body:
Yves, any plans this weekend? Ta mère's at her sister's in Sacramento Saturday so it'll just be me here. I was thinking of firing up the grill if the weather cooperates — you and Sienna are welcome anytime if you're not booked.

Nothing fancy, just whatever's in the fridge.

Papa
snippet: Yves, any plans this weekend? Ta mère's at her sister's in Sacramento Saturda...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
Saturday works perfectly — Sienna's off after her lunch shift, so we'll both come by. We'll bring dessert; I've got a sugar pie that needs an audience.

What time are you thinking? We can be there by 4:30 if that works.

Yves
snippet: Saturday works perfectly — Sienna's off after her lunch shift, so we'll both ...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
4:30 is perfect. Tell Sienna I finally got the good charcoal she likes, and I'll save her the corner chair on the porch.

See you both Saturday.

Papa
snippet: 4:30 is perfect. Tell Sienna I finally got the good charcoal she likes, and I...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

Patrick
